title: Easter Night
content:
All night had shout of men and cry
                     Of woeful women filled His way;
         Until that noon of sombre sky
                     On Friday, clamour and display
         Smote Him; no solitude had He,
         No silence, since Gethsemane.

         Public was Death; but Power, but Might,
                     But Life again, but Victory,
         Were hushed within the dead of night,
                     The shutter�d dark, the secrecy.
         And all alone, alone, alone
         He rose again behind the stone.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Alice Christiana Gertrude Thompson Meynell delivers a powerful performance in "Easter Night"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
